summary: O'Neil provides a behind-the-scenes update on the editing process for her upcoming book 'Weapons of Math Destruction.' She walks through the various stages of book production—from content edits to line editing to galleys to printing overseas. The post serves as both an explanation for reduced blog activity and an honest reflection on why books, despite being 'the opposite of sexy,' benefit from rigorous editing. She announces a tentative September 2016 launch date.
key_points: Book editing involves multiple distinct phases: content edits, line editing (commas, formatting), galleys, and cover design
The production timeline from editing to printed books takes months, including overseas printing and shipping
The careful, tedious editing process is precisely what makes books better than blogs
The book's full title reveals its thesis: 'how big data increases inequality and threatens democracy'
money_quotes: Right now I'm eyeball deep in line edits for my book, Weapons of Math Destruction: how big data increases inequality and threatens democracy.

my brain is completely occupied with this stuff, which is the opposite of sexy but on the other hand is exactly why the book will be better (hopefully!) than a blog – it will be actually carefully edited.
they print a bunch of copies in China and have it all shipped back here by boat. The process takes months and it's all new to me.
key_insight: The unglamorous, painstaking work of careful editing is what elevates a book above a blog post.
